execution_summary: Built an R-based Docker environment (rocker/tidyverse:4.3.1) with required packages and pandoc. Started the container and planned to run the entrypoint analysis.Rmd. Before execution, I fixed a minor Rmd inline reference bug (h0.test.print -> h0.test.result), updated replication_info.json to mount replication_data to /app/data and include jsonlite, and created a wrapper analysis.Rmd at repo root. The first run failed because analysis.Rmd (an Rmd file) was treated as a bash script. I replaced the wrapper with a small bash script that calls rmarkdown::render on the real Rmd (replication_data/analysis.Rmd), keeping the orchestrator entry intact. The re-run succeeded, generating analysis.nb.html and analysis-derived.csv. Results support the preregistered hypothesis: 1D test accuracy is higher in the contingent condition than in the full-information condition, with a statistically significant positive difference and medium effect size.

code_executed.logs: Approved for execution. Initial attempt failed due to entry analysis.Rmd being treated as a shell-executable with Rmd content. Wrapper was rewritten to a bash script that internally calls rmarkdown::render on the child Rmd.
code_executed.environment: Rocker tidyverse 4.3.1 with pandoc; R packages: dplyr, readr, tidyr, ggplot2, stringr, jsonlite, rmarkdown, knitr

code_executed.command: bash analysis.Rmd  # wrapper script calling: R -q -e "rmarkdown::render('replication_data/analysis.Rmd', output_dir = '/app/data', output_file = 'analysis.html')"
code_executed.logs: Render steps 1–25 completed. Output created: /app/data/analysis.nb.html. Summary stats and figures generated. No runtime errors. One ggplot2 deprecation warning for ..count.. notation.
code_executed.environment: R 4.3.1 (rocker/tidyverse) with dplyr, readr, tidyr, ggplot2, stringr, jsonlite, rmarkdown, knitr; pandoc

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants in the contingent-information condition will have a higher one-dimensional (1D) strategy score at test than participants in the full-information condition (mean acc_1d_contingent > mean acc_1d_full).

tables.table_description: Group means (M, SD), independent-samples t-test result, and Cohen's d reported in the rendered analysis.
CSV of derived/filtered data used by analysis for reproducibility.

figures.figure_description: Stacked area showing proportions of 1D, 2D, and None strategies across blocks by condition.
Histogram comparing per-subject 1D test accuracy in contingent vs full-information conditions.
